name: Ber
seedTip: Soak seed in room-temperature water for 24 hours before planting.
notes: Exceptional heat and drought tolerance. Thrives in clay-heavy soils. Produces nutritious edible fruit.

name: Shisham
seedTip: Direct sow during monsoon. No pre-treatment needed.
notes: King of Punjab hardwoods. Fixes nitrogen. Extremely valuable timber tree.

name: Neem
seedTip: Plant fresh seed — viability drops within weeks of harvest.
notes: Natural pest repellent, medicinal uses. Extremely heat tolerant. Fast germination.

name: Jamun
seedTip: Sow fresh seed immediately — never let it dry out or viability collapses.
notes: Large edible dark berries. Becomes a massive shade tree over decades.

name: Mulberry
seedTip: Stratify seeds in moist sand for 30 days in the fridge before spring planting in cold zones.
notes: Edible fruit and leaves (silkworm feed). Very adaptable across climates. Fast growing.

name: Kikar (Babul)
seedTip: Nick the seed coat with a blade or soak in hot water for 10 min to break dormancy.
notes: Deepest roots of any local tree. Thorny. Nitrogen fixer. Survives total drought neglect.

name: Peepal
seedTip: Seeds are tiny — surface sow on moist soil, do not bury. Keep humid until germination.
notes: Longest living tree in South Asia. Roots are very aggressive — plant 5m+ from any structure.

name: Olive
seedTip: Stratify seeds in cold-moist conditions for 3 months. Germination is slow and erratic — be patient.
notes: Extraordinary longevity. Drought-proof once established. Fruit, oil, and timber value.

name: Cork Oak
seedTip: Plant acorns immediately after collection. Sow 3–5 cm deep. Taproot forms fast.
notes: Harvested every 9 years for cork without killing the tree. Exceptional fire resistance.

name: Carob
seedTip: Scarify seed with sandpaper or soak in hot water 24 hrs to break the hard coat.
notes: Edible pods used as chocolate substitute. Extraordinary drought resistance. Deep taproot.

name: Eucalyptus
seedTip: Tiny seeds — surface sow on moist mix, press lightly, do not cover. Germinates in 7–14 days.
notes: Extremely fast growing. Used for timber, windbreaks, oil. Can drain soil moisture heavily.

name: Baobab
seedTip: Scarify seed heavily with sandpaper, then soak 24 hrs. Germination can take 2–6 months.
notes: The tree of life. Edible fruit, leaves, bark. Stores thousands of litres of water in its trunk.

name: Umbrella Thorn Acacia
seedTip: Soak in hot water or boiling water briefly, then let cool and soak 24 hrs.
notes: Iconic African savanna tree. Nitrogen fixer. Shade and fodder. Highly drought resistant.

name: English Oak
seedTip: Plant acorns in autumn immediately after collection, 5 cm deep. Cold winter triggers spring germination.
notes: One of Europe's most important biodiversity trees. Supports 500+ species. Legendary lifespan.

name: Silver Birch
seedTip: Surface sow in spring. Seeds need light to germinate — do not cover. Keep moist.
notes: Pioneer species, fast establishing. Beautiful bark. Shorter lived than oaks but rapid canopy.

name: Scots Pine
seedTip: Cold-stratify for 30–60 days in damp paper in the fridge, then sow in early spring.
notes: Extremely cold and wind hardy. Deep taproot. Iconic silhouette. Thrives in poor sandy soils.

name: Pecan
seedTip: Cold-stratify nuts for 90–120 days. Plant in deep containers — taproot grows fast and long.
notes: Edible nuts. Deep taproot makes it wind resistant. Slow to first fruit (6–10 yrs from seed).

name: Bald Cypress
seedTip: Cold-stratify for 90 days. Can tolerate flooding — one of very few conifers that does.
notes: Extraordinary storm and flood resistance. Grows in wet or dry soil. Exceptional longevity.

name: Siberian Larch
seedTip: Cold-stratify 30 days. Sow in early spring. Very frost hardy — one of the toughest conifers alive.
notes: Survives −60°C. Deciduous conifer. Extremely durable timber. Thrives where other trees cannot.
